title: Model Assurance Framework Mapping Catalog
description: Authoritative catalog of the 10 frameworks used in modelverifier.ai mappings. Each entry defines: source metadata (version, authority, normative force, license), the allowed requirement_id pattern for validation, the requirement_id enumeration where the set is bounded (llm10), coverage guidance, and known mapping caveats. The build pipeline (validate:sources, audit:mappings) reads this catalog to validate all mapping entries in control records.

nist_rmf.title: NIST Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework 1.0
nist_rmf.authority: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
companion_documents: NIST AI 100-4 (Reducing Risks Posed by Synthetic Content, 2024)

structure.description: Four core functions: GOVERN (culture, policies, processes), MAP (context and categorization), MEASURE (analysis and assessment), MANAGE (risk treatment and improvement). Each function has numbered categories (e.g., GOVERN-1) and subcategories (e.g., GOVERN-1.1).

nist_rmf.coverage_guidance: Strong alignment with OA (governance), EV (measurement), and CR (manage/improve) layers. MAP aligns to LI and EV-09 (applicability). MEASURE aligns to monitoring and evaluation controls. MANAGE aligns to incident response and continuous improvement.
mapping_caveats: NIST AI RMF is voluntary and not a compliance certification — do not imply mandatory compliance in mapping rationale.
The Playbook subcategories are action-oriented guidance, not normative requirements — map to core framework categories when in doubt.
NIST AI 100-4 (synthetic content) is a companion document, not part of the RMF proper — cite it separately when relevant to BH-09.

iso_42001.title: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 — Information technology — Artificial intelligence — Management system
companion_documents: ISO/IEC 24029 series — Robustness of neural networks
ISO/IEC 5259 series — Data quality for analytics and ML

structure.description: Plan-Do-Check-Act management system structure. Main body clauses 4 (Context) through 10 (Improvement). Normative Annex A provides AI-specific controls A.2 through A.10. Designed for third-party certification.

iso_42001.coverage_guidance: Broad coverage across all six layers. A.3 (AI system impact assessment) aligns to EV-09 and LI. A.6 (AI system data) aligns to TG. A.7 (AI system development) aligns to EV. A.8 (AI system lifecycle) aligns to BH and CR. A.9 (human oversight) aligns to OA.
mapping_caveats: ISO 42001 is a management system standard — many requirements are about having a documented process, not about technical control outcomes. Distinguish process requirements from technical control requirements in rationale.
License is paid/proprietary — do not reproduce requirement text. Map by requirement ID and independent rationale only.
Companion standards (42005, 23894, 5338) are separate documents; cite them explicitly with their own source entries.

eu_ai_act.title: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 of the European Parliament and of the Council — Artificial Intelligence Act
eu_ai_act.authority: European Union — European Parliament and Council

enforcement_timeline.timeline_note: European Parliament approved revised high-risk enforcement dates June 16, 2026. Standalone high-risk Dec 2, 2027; product-embedded Aug 2, 2028. Final legal status requires Council adoption and publication in Official Journal. Track before publishing obligations with these dates.

structure.description: Twelve chapters and thirteen annexes. Key high-risk requirements: Art-9 (risk management), Art-10 (data governance), Art-11 (technical documentation), Art-12 (record-keeping), Art-13 (transparency), Art-14 (human oversight), Art-15 (accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity). GPAI obligations: Art-51 through Art-56. Annex III: high-risk use case list.

eu_ai_act.coverage_guidance: Art-9 (risk management) aligns to EV-09, OA, CR. Art-10 (data governance) aligns to TG. Art-11 (technical documentation) aligns to LI-04. Art-12 (record-keeping) aligns to BH-05, CR. Art-13 (transparency) aligns to LI and OA. Art-14 (human oversight) aligns to OA-02. Art-15 (accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity) aligns to EV and BH.
mapping_caveats: CRITICAL: Always specify effective_from in the obligation object. High-risk Annex III provisions do not apply until Dec 2, 2027 (standalone) or Aug 2, 2028 (product-embedded). Mapping a current control to Art-9 does not imply the obligation is currently enforceable.

EU AI Act classification is use-case and actor-role dependent — not an intrinsic model property. The eu_ai_act_classification in assurance_target must be formally determined before mapping to high-risk provisions.
Do not map to prohibited AI practices (Art-5) unless the control is specifically about preventing prohibited use.
GPAI obligations (Art-51+) apply to GPAI model providers, not necessarily to deployers.

sr262.title: SR 26-2 — Supervisory Guidance on Model Risk Management
sr262.authority: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
companion_documents: OCC Bulletin 2026-13 (interagency companion — also supervisory guidance, not binding)
sr262.applicability_note: Applies to all supervised financial institutions. Heightened expectations for institutions with $30B+ in consolidated assets. OCC 2026-13 explicitly states: guidance does not establish enforceable standards; noncompliance alone will not result in supervisory criticism.

structure.description: Main sections covering: model definition and inventory, model risk taxonomy, model development and implementation, validation (conceptual soundness, ongoing monitoring, outcomes analysis), governance and controls. Appendices on specific model types and AI/ML considerations.

sr262.coverage_guidance: Strong alignment with LI (model inventory), EV (independent validation), OA (governance), BH (ongoing monitoring), CR (outcomes analysis). S-3 (validation) aligns closely to EV-01, EV-06, EV-07, EV-09. S-4 (governance) aligns to OA-01, OA-07.
mapping_caveats: CRITICAL: SR 26-2 is supervisory guidance, not binding law. Always set legal_status: 'guidance' in obligation objects. Do not represent compliance with SR 26-2 as legal compliance.
SR 26-2 supersedes SR 11-7 and SR 21-8 — do not cite the superseded letters as sources.
OCC 2026-13 is separate guidance with separate authority — cite it as a distinct source, not as part of SR 26-2.
Applicability is heightened for $30B+ institutions — use the asset_threshold_usd field in assurance_target trigger conditions.

aisvs.title: OWASP AI Security Verification Standard v1.0
aisvs.version_pinning_note: No formal GitHub release tag at time of initial authoring (June 24, 2026 release). Pin source_version to the commit hash from which mappings were made. Update version pinning when a tagged release is published. Do NOT treat as 'final' without verifying current repository state.
aisvs.coverage_guidance: C1 aligns to LI and OA. C2 aligns to TG. C3-C4 align to TG and EV. C5 aligns to EV. C6 aligns to BH. C7 aligns to BH and CR. C8 aligns to EV-04 (red team), BH-04, and cross-domain security controls. C9 aligns to TG-03. C10 aligns to OA and LI-09.
mapping_caveats: CRITICAL LICENSE: AISVS is CC BY-SA 4.0 (ShareAlike). If this control matrix is published under a non-SA license (e.g., CC BY-NC 4.0), requirement text must NOT be copied verbatim — map by requirement_id and independent rationale only.
Pin source_version to a commit hash since there is no formal release tag at initial authoring time.
Chapter organization may change between commits — verify requirement_id stability before each version bump.

llm10.title: OWASP Top 10 for Large Language Model Applications 2025
llm10.coverage_guidance: LLM01 aligns to EV-04 (injection resistance evaluation) — enforcement owned by securitycontrols.ai. LLM03 aligns to TG supply chain controls. LLM04 aligns to TG-04 (data poisoning). LLM06 aligns to OA-04 (autonomy and agency controls). LLM09 aligns to EV-02 (misinformation / factual accuracy evaluation). LLM10 aligns to BH rate and cost controls.
mapping_caveats: CRITICAL: LLM09:2025 is Misinformation, not concept drift or statistical distribution shift. Do not map performance drift controls to LLM09.
CRITICAL: LLM03:2025 is Supply Chain, not Training Data Poisoning. Training data poisoning is LLM04:2025.
This framework covers LLM application security risks, not general model governance. Only map when the control specifically addresses LLM-oriented threats.
Model Assurance owns injection-resistance evaluation and measured failure rates. securitycontrols.ai owns runtime detection, isolation, and enforcement — do not duplicate ownership.

aicm.title: Cloud Security Alliance AI Controls Matrix v1.1

domain_prefixes._v11_note: AICM v1.1 expanded from 10 to 18 domains with 247 total control objectives. New domain prefixes from the 8 additional domains require verification against the v1.1 document before use in control mappings.

aicm.coverage_guidance: GOV aligns to OA. TG (AICM) aligns to TG layer. MG aligns to LI and EV. RMG aligns to EV-09 and CR. SEC aligns to EV-04 and BH. OPS aligns to BH and CR. AUD aligns to BH-05 and CR. AICM v1.1 added 8 new domains — review domain alignment after full v1.1 document retrieval and ID validation.
mapping_caveats: CSA AICM domain prefix 'TG' conflicts with the modelverifier.ai layer prefix 'TG'. In mapping rationale, always qualify whether TG refers to the CSA AICM domain or the modelverifier.ai layer.
CSA AISMM (AI Safety Management Model) is a separate CSA artifact — do not cite AICM controls as AISMM references.

Existing control mappings in controls/ reference AICM v1.0.3 control IDs (published 2024-06-01). AICM v1.1 released 2026-06-22 expanded to 247 objectives across 18 domains. Validate all existing AICM control ID mappings against the v1.1 document before the next authoring cycle; flag any IDs not present in v1.1 as stale.

mitre.title: MITRE ATLAS — Adversarial Threat Landscape for Artificial Intelligence Systems

id_type_guide.AML.T: Technique — adversarial action an attacker takes against an ML system
id_type_guide.AML.M: Mitigation — countermeasure against one or more techniques
id_type_guide.AML.DS: Data Source — observable that provides evidence of technique use

mitre.coverage_guidance: AML.T0020 (Poison Training Data) aligns to TG-04. AML.T0051 (Prompt Injection) aligns to EV-04 evaluation of injection resistance — runtime enforcement owned by securitycontrols.ai. AML.T0024 (Exfiltration via Inference API) aligns to BH-05 and cross-domain security controls. AML.T0044 (Full ML Model Access) aligns to LI-06 (artifact integrity), BH-05, and security domain access controls. AML.T0018 (Backdoor) aligns to TG-04 and EV-04.
mapping_caveats: CRITICAL: AML.T0051 is LLM Prompt Injection, NOT capability-threshold crossing. Do not map frontier capability evaluation controls to AML.T0051.
Capability-threshold crossing has no direct ATLAS technique as of v5.6.0 — document as 'frontier assurance gap' with fit: 'none' and a rationale explaining the absence.
ATLAS is a threat taxonomy, not a control framework — mappings describe the threat a control mitigates, not a requirement the control satisfies. Adjust mapping rationale language accordingly.
Version 5.5.0 was released February 2026; 5.6.0 was released May 4, 2026. Always cite 5.6.0 or later.
MITRE ATLAS covers technique-level threat mapping. securitycontrols.ai owns runtime threat detection and prevention; Model Assurance owns evaluation of resistance to these techniques.

nist_ai_600_1.title: Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework: Generative Artificial Intelligence Profile
nist_ai_600_1.authority: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
companion_documents: NIST AI 100-1 (AI RMF 1.0, Jan 2023) — parent framework
NIST AI 100-4 (Reducing Risks Posed by Synthetic Content, March 2024)

structure.description: Twelve GenAI-specific risk categories, each mapped to the four AI RMF functions (GOVERN, MAP, MEASURE, MANAGE). Requirement IDs in this catalog use the risk category name as a stable section-level identifier. Finer-grained action references use the rationale and source_locator fields.
risk_categories: CBRN — Chemical, Biological, Radiological, Nuclear capability uplift
CONFABULATION — hallucination, false or ungrounded model outputs
DATA-PRIVACY — personal data memorization, membership inference, inference-time exposure
HUMAN-AI-CONFIG — over-reliance, automation bias, degraded human oversight
INFO-INTEGRITY — disinformation, synthetic media, misleading AI-generated content at scale
INFO-SECURITY — adversarial attacks, prompt injection, supply chain, exfiltration
IP — intellectual property, copyright, training data rights violations
OBSCENE-DEGRADING — harmful, abusive, non-consensual, or exploitative content

nist_ai_600_1.coverage_guidance: Primary alignment to EV layer (evaluation controls for GenAI risks) and BH layer (runtime behavior). CONFABULATION maps to EV-02 (GenAI evaluation). CBRN maps to EV-03 (dangerous capability). INFO-SECURITY maps to EV-04 (red-team) and BH-04 (runtime injection monitoring). INFO-INTEGRITY maps to BH-09 (synthetic content provenance). DATA-PRIVACY maps to TG-03 (training data). IP maps to TG-01 (data authorization). HUMAN-AI-CONFIG maps to OA-02 (meaningful human oversight).
mapping_caveats: NIST AI 600-1 is a voluntary companion to AI RMF 1.0, not a separate compliance certification.

Requirement IDs in this catalog reference risk category names. Source locators should reference the document section (e.g., section: 'CONFABULATION'). Full action-level granularity (GV-1.1, etc.) will be added in a future mapping pass.
Many controls address multiple 600-1 risk categories. Map to the primary category; note secondary categories in rationale.
Runtime enforcement of 600-1 risks (prompt injection detection, output filtering) is owned by securitycontrols.ai. Model Assurance owns evaluation of resistance and measurement of risk.

owasp_aitg.title: OWASP AI Testing Guide
companion_documents: OWASP AISVS v1.0 (AI Security Verification Standard)

structure.description: Structured testing methodology for AI trustworthiness across security, robustness, fairness, and explainability dimensions. Maps to evaluation (EV), deployment (BH), and continuous assurance (CR) layers.

owasp_aitg.coverage_guidance: Primary alignment to EV layer (structured evaluation and testing methodology) and BH/CR layers (runtime testing and evidence). Complements AISVS requirements with specific testing procedures. Full systematic mapping to controls is in progress.
mapping_caveats: OWASP AI Testing Guide is CC BY-SA 4.0 (ShareAlike). Do not reproduce requirement text verbatim — map by identifier and write independent rationale, same as for AISVS.
Full systematic mapping to modelverifier.ai controls is in progress. Initial mappings use broad category references.
OWASP AI Testing Guide requirement IDs use the AITG-XX-N format. Verify IDs against the canonical document before mapping.

validation_rules.description: Rules enforced by the build pipeline audit:mappings step for this domain.

rules.description: Every mapping entry must have a requirement_id matching the framework's allowed pattern.
Every mapping with fit 'direct' must include a source_locator with at least a 'section' field.
Every mapping with fit 'partial' must include a non-empty uncovered_portion.
Every mapping rationale must be at least 30 characters.
llm10 requirement_id must exactly match one of the 10 allowed IDs.
eu_ai_act mappings must have a corresponding obligation object with legal_status and effective_from.
sr262 mappings must have legal_status: 'guidance' in their corresponding obligation — never 'binding'.
mitre mappings with fit 'none' are permitted when no ATLAS technique applies (frontier gap). Rationale must explain the absence.
Coverage: each published control must have mappings to at least nist_rmf and iso_42001.
source_version must be set on every mapping and must match the framework version in this catalog.
